mnemonics: RIP Thread: A thread is a path of execution within a program. Remember it as a RIP (Runnable In Program) to its destination.
keyPoints: Threads are lightweight sub-processes that share the same memory space.
There are two main ways to create a thread: extending the Thread class or implementing the Runnable interface.
The JVM gives each thread its own call stack for method invocations and local variables.
commonMistakes: Calling run() directly instead of start(), which doesn't launch a new thread of execution.
Not synchronizing access to shared resources, leading to race conditions and inconsistent data.
Creating too many threads, which can lead to high memory consumption and excessive context switching, degrading performance.
